auditor_notes: Hybrid: SHV-5 ESBL + SHV_C-112A promoter-up overexpression + dual ompK35/36 frameshift. Permeability loss is the primary trigger; ESBL hyperexpression provides the residual hydrolytic activity that pushes carbapenem MIC into R range.
Same isolate (573.24407). Ertapenem is the most porin-sensitive carbapenem so permeability is dominant; SHV-5 hyperexpression is the secondary contribution.
Same isolate. Hybrid mechanism. Imipenem MIC bumped to R by combined porin loss + SHV-5 hyperexpression.
Same isolate. Hybrid mechanism. Meropenem the least sensitive of the carbapenems but still pushed to R by combined mechanism.

Tri-vendor invariant disagreement (GPT-5.4 + Gemini 3.1 Pro both predicted enzymatic_inactivation against the heuristic intrinsic label). Mechanistically fosA is a Mn2+/K+-dependent glutathione-S-transferase that enzymatically conjugates fosfomycin; the heuristic conflated chromosomal/intrinsic acquisition status with mechanism class. Adjudication adopts the molecular mechanism class.
Same fosA adjudication. Vendor consensus + biochemistry concur on enzymatic mechanism; intrinsic axis is acquisition status, not mechanism.

Hybrid amikacin R. Visible evidence has both 16S rRNA methyltransferase ArmA (target_modification) and aminoglycoside-modifying enzymes AAC(6')-Ib3 + APH(3')-VI (enzymatic_inactivation). ArmA confers high-level pan-aminoglycoside resistance via ribosome methylation and dominates clinically when present; AMEs are the secondary additive driver. Tri-vendor consensus on target_modification.
